_note: Real numbers from the README benchmark table (single-level fast loop vs + meta recursion). Reviewed by: python -m paperforge review --repo ../metaskill-evolve
title: MetaSkill-Evolve — two-timescale recursive skill evolution
hypothesis: The fast loop is the workhorse; recursion helps only in a narrow procedure-bottleneck regime. On near-saturated tasks (ALFWorld ~92%) and strong backbones the meta-gain vanishes (+0.00). These are EN benchmarks at this eval size — whether the recursion gain transfers to Vietnamese tasks or holds at larger eval scale is untested.

claims.claim: Recursive (two-level) skill evolution beats the single-level fast loop on a reasoning-heavy benchmark.
claims.regime: OfficeQA, single-level fast loop vs two-level recursion
claims.notes: +6.38 over single-level (which itself is +17.16 over no-skill — the fast loop does most of the work).

claims.claim: The recursion gain also holds on a search-bottlenecked benchmark.
claims.regime: SealQA, single-level fast loop vs two-level recursion
claims.notes: +8.05 — the largest recursion gain, a genuine procedure-bottleneck regime.

claims.claim: The recursion gain generalizes to embodied, near-saturated tasks.
claims.metric: ALFWorld success rate, + meta (recursion) vs single-level
claims.regime: ALFWorld, single-level fast loop vs two-level recursion
claims.notes: +1.92 on an already-saturated task (~92%) — the meta-gain is within noise here, matching the meta-gain-vs-strength law (+0.50 weak / +0.25 mid / +0.00 strong).
